context.story_engine: Every week a diagnostician solves a medical mystery, testing the limits of ethics and truth

plotlines.name: House: Case of the Week
plotlines.goal: solve medical mysteries that other doctors cannot
plotlines.obstacle: incomplete information, patient lies, time pressure, rare conditions

plotlines.name: House: Authority
plotlines.goal: maintain diagnostic independence and avoid administrative duties
plotlines.obstacle: Cuddy's institutional demands, Vogler's corporate control
plotlines.stakes: loss of diagnostic freedom, forced conformity

plotlines.name: House: Addiction
plotlines.goal: manage chronic pain while maintaining professional function
plotlines.obstacle: Vicodin dependency, colleagues' concern, withdrawal symptoms

plotlines.name: House: Love
plotlines.obstacle: her marriage to Mark, past betrayal, emotional walls
plotlines.stakes: emotional isolation, lost chance at happiness

plotlines.name: Wilson: Friendship
plotlines.goal: support House while maintaining his own principles

plotlines.name: Chase: Loyalty
plotlines.goal: secure his position on House's team
plotlines.obstacle: Vogler's manipulation, team dynamics, father issues

plotlines.name: Foreman: Identity
plotlines.goal: establish himself as a competent diagnostician
plotlines.obstacle: House's dominance, personal biases, career choices

plotlines.name: Vogler: Control
plotlines.goal: reshape the hospital according to corporate efficiency
plotlines.obstacle: House's resistance, board politics, institutional culture
plotlines.stakes: hundred million dollar investment, corporate reputation

events.event: Rebecca Adler collapses in her classroom with sudden aphasia and seizure
Wilson brings Adler's case to House, claiming she is his cousin
House assigns the case to his three-person team
Adler has allergic reaction to gadolinium during MRI, Cameron performs emergency tracheotomy
House diagnoses cerebral vasculitis and starts steroids
Adler seizes again and goes into cardiac arrest, disproving vasculitis theory
House orders Foreman and Cameron to break into Adler's apartment
Foreman objects on ethical grounds, House insists everybody lies
Team finds opened ham package, House suspects neurocysticercosis
X-ray of Adler's thigh reveals calcified cysts, confirming tapeworm larvae
Adler refuses treatment because she distrusts House
Wilson persuades Adler by admitting he lied about being her cousin
Cuddy pressures House to work clinic shifts per his contract
House evades clinic duty with creative excuses
Cuddy revokes House's diagnostics privileges until he takes clinic patients
House grudgingly treats orange man with carrot and niacin excess
House's chronic leg pain and Vicodin reliance are introduced
House tells Cameron he hired her because she is extremely attractive

episodes.theme: the necessity of deception in healing

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters lying or manipulating others for what they believe is a greater good
Wilson's lie about being Adler's cousin serves both the medical case and establishes his willingness to bend ethics for House

events.event: Dan is admitted with double vision and night terrors after lacrosse head injury
House notices involuntary myoclonic jerk in Dan's foot, unrelated to head trauma
Cameron fabricates a referral letter to ensure House sees the patient
Team finds large blockage in brain ventricle and relieves pressure
Dan is found sleepwalking on hospital roof, hallucinating lacrosse field
Cameron proposes neurosyphilis diagnosis
Dan suffers auditory hallucination during lumbar puncture, eliminating neurosyphilis
House makes bet that Dan's parents are not both biologically his
House secretly runs DNA test using parents' coffee cups
DNA test reveals neither parent is genetically related to Dan
House deduces measles exposure without maternal antibodies led to brain infection
Retinal biopsy confirms measles virus in brain tissue
Dan receives intraventricular interferon and recovers
Dan reveals he already knew he was adopted from his cleft chin
Wilson tells House he enjoys being right more than helping patients
Cuddy pushes House on clinic duty, House resists

episodes.theme: the necessity of uncomfortable truths

interactions.description: all storylines explore how truth-telling, even when painful, serves a greater purpose
Cameron's romantic feelings drive her to bend rules to get House involved in the case

events.event: Brandon Merrell collapses after sex with multiple symptoms
Chase, Cameron, and Foreman invoke Occam's Razor for single cause
House proposes two co-existing conditions against team's reasoning
Antibiotics shut down Brandon's kidneys, disproving hypotheses
Brandon's condition follows unpredictable pattern of improvement and deterioration
House notices hair loss and realizes colchicine poisoning pattern
Chase investigates pharmacy, pharmacist denies error
New prescription bottle reveals different pills
House confronts Brandon's mother who admits giving leftover pills
Pharmacy error confirmed, Brandon recovers
Cuddy demands House perform clinic duty
House argues diagnostic puzzles are better use of time than clinic
Foreman challenges House's reasoning openly
Cameron mediates between House and team
Chase defers to House's authority
Wilson serves as sounding board for House's case frustration
House's Vicodin use noticed but unremarked by colleagues

episodes.theme: the complexity of truth versus the appeal of simplicity

interactions.description: all storylines explore the tension between simple solutions and complex realities — medical diagnosis, administrative demands, and professional hierarchy
Foreman's challenge to House's methodology directly impacts the case resolution

events.event: Newborn Maxine Hartig develops vomiting, lethargy, fever, then seizes in maternity ward
House discovers a second newborn with fever and suspects an epidemic
Cuddy dismisses House's epidemic theory, saying two sick babies aren't proof
Third and fourth infants develop identical symptoms, Cuddy orders maternity ward closed
Foreman identifies both echovirus 11 and CMV but cannot determine which causes illness
House starts empirical treatment with vancomycin and aztreonam, orders MRIs
House proposes testing mothers' antibodies to identify which virus they lack immunity to
Antibody results point to echovirus 11 as the culprit
Two infants develop simultaneous kidney failure
House makes agonizing decision to take each baby off different antibiotic, knowing one will likely die
Baby Boy Chen-Lupino dies but other baby's response identifies effective treatment
Remaining infected newborns recover with experimental antiviral
House traces epidemic source to elderly hospital volunteer carrying virus asymptomatically
Cuddy grudgingly recognizes House's brutal triage decisions saved the majority despite the cost
Foreman tells Hartig parents full severity of situation
Cameron gives softer account to other family, avoiding harsh realities
Wilson criticizes Cameron for not preparing family for worst outcome
Chase executes orders efficiently but avoids ethical debate
House acknowledges cost of calculated gamble privately but defends it publicly

episodes.theme: the cost of saving lives

interactions.description: all storylines explore different aspects of moral responsibility — House's brutal calculus, Cuddy's administrative burden, Cameron's protective instincts, Wilson's ethical standards
Cuddy's initial dismissal of House's epidemic theory merges with the medical crisis, forcing her to acknowledge his methods

events.event: Sister Augustine arrives with hand rash, House diagnoses contact dermatitis from dish soap
Augustine suffers severe asthma attack, House administers epinephrine causing cardiac arrest
Cuddy accuses House of giving ten times the appropriate dose, threatens malpractice case
Team tests for Churg-Strauss vasculitis with negative results
Augustine has seizure and tests positive for herpetic encephalitis
Foreman goes to Cuddy unconvinced by House's theories
Cuddy removes House as attending physician and takes the case herself
House visits monastery and shares tea with Mother Superior, learns Augustine's street history
House demonstrates figwort tea interaction with epinephrine, clearing malpractice accusation
Augustine moved to clean room, experiences crisis of faith
Chase counsels Augustine about faith, revealing his seminary background
Wilson's offhand remark about 'God inside her' leads House to consider internal allergen
House orders full-body scan revealing decades-old copper IUD
House realizes copper IUD plus copper pots created cumulative allergic overload
IUD surgically removed, Augustine recovers in time for Christmas
Wilson spends Christmas with House instead of wife, mentions she's 'used to being alone'
House's Vicodin use is visible but uncommented upon by team

episodes.theme: hidden truths and their consequences

interactions.description: all storylines involve concealed information that creates problems when revealed - Augustine's past, House's dosage mistake, Wilson's marital troubles
the malpractice threat directly impacts House's ability to solve the case, forcing him to work outside official channels
Christmas setting provides ironic counterpoint to themes of faith, redemption, and hidden sins

events.event: Lucy Palmeiro collapses at unemployment office with severe leg pain
Lucas secretly gives Lucy vodka to calm her down before respiratory arrest
ER diagnoses pulmonary embolism from deep vein thrombosis, attributes clotting to alcoholism
House overhears the case and takes it, skeptical of schizophrenia diagnosis
Blood tests reveal prolonged prothrombin time suggesting vitamin K deficiency
Chase performs ultrasound and finds 5.8-centimeter cancerous liver tumor
Surgical team refuses to operate on tumor that large
House and Wilson devise ethanol embolization scheme to shrink tumor
House and Wilson present shrunken tumor as newly scanned to deceive surgeon
Cuddy discovers the deception but allows surgery to proceed
House discovers Lucas is only fifteen, not eighteen as claimed
House realizes Lucy called social services herself, showing rational behavior
Cameron suggests Wilson's disease as diagnosis
Team examines Lucy's eyes and finds Kayser-Fleischer rings confirming Wilson's disease
Treatment resolves both physical symptoms and apparent schizophrenia
Lucy is discharged home with her son

episodes.theme: the danger of misdiagnosis and the courage to question authority

interactions.description: both storylines explore challenging established medical authority — questioning the schizophrenia diagnosis parallels defying surgical protocols
Wilson's participation in the embolization scheme merges his loyalty to House with the medical case and defiance of hospital rules

patches.reason: no events in this episode advance House's pain management storyline
no events in this episode involve Stacy or House's romantic feelings
no events in this episode involve Wilson's marriage
no events in this episode specifically advance Chase's goal of securing his position
no events in this episode involve Vogler or corporate efficiency

events.event: Elise Snow exhibits alarming symptoms: sleeping 18 hours daily, fever, and striking her husband without provocation
Cameron brings the case to House after six doctors fail to diagnose Elise
House initially dismisses the case as clinical depression
Cameron points out elevated sed rate indicating inflammation, ruling out depression
House suspects brain tumor and orders scans, but imaging reveals nothing
Team treats for possible breast cancer but Elise develops seizures and hallucinations
Foreman suggests tularemia because Elise could have been exposed at work
House narrows differential to tularemia versus African trypanosomiasis
House pressures Ed about whether Elise has ever been unfaithful
Elise slips into a coma before the infidelity question can be resolved
House starts treatment with melarsoprol for sleeping sickness despite the risks
Elise emerges from coma, confirming sleeping sickness diagnosis and her infidelity
Cameron informs Adam that Elise's diagnosis confirms the affair
House notices Wilson wearing a new green tie and nice shoes
House deduces Wilson is attracted to someone at the hospital
Wilson initially denies attraction, then claims to have taken a nurse to lunch
House needles Wilson throughout the episode about his evasiveness
Cameron reveals she was married for six months to a man who died of thyroid cancer
House files away Cameron's revelation about marrying a terminally ill man

episodes.theme: the lies we tell ourselves and others about fidelity

interactions.description: all three storylines explore infidelity and deception — Elise's affair, Wilson's attraction, and Cameron's revelation about her marriage
House pressures the patient's husband about marital honesty while simultaneously exposing Wilson's own marital deception

events.event: Matt Davis collapses during AP calculus exam with nausea, abdominal pain, and severe bradycardia
Foreman convinces House to take the case by claiming he has ruled out obvious diagnoses
House suspects drug use and sends Cameron and Foreman to search Matt's home
Cameron discovers jar of tomato sauce with popped lid suggesting contamination
House diagnoses organophosphate poisoning and orders pralidoxime treatment
Margo refuses treatment, demanding second opinion and losing faith in House
Cuddy tells House he cannot get court order simply because mother disagrees
House has Cuddy present condescending consent form to manipulate Margo into accepting treatment
Chi Ling admitted with identical symptoms, living ten miles away with no connection to Matt
Team discovers both families purchased TKO laundry detergent
Chi's mother reveals son wore brand-new never-washed clothes, ruling out detergent
House and Chase test discarded clothing fabric, finding phosdrin contamination
Poisoning traced to truck selling stolen clothes contaminated with agricultural pesticides
Both boys recover after targeted treatment
Elderly woman Georgia brought to clinic by son worried about her good mood

episodes.theme: the limits of parental authority versus medical expertise

interactions.description: both storylines explore the tension between medical knowledge and patient autonomy, with House's diagnostic certainty challenged by parental rights
the medical mystery cannot be solved until House navigates the authority conflict with Margo

patches.reason: recurring clinic subplot with Georgia provides comic relief but doesn't fit existing storylines

events.event: John Henry Giles collapses during recording session, stops breathing
Dr. Hamilton attributes episode to pneumonia complicating ALS
House doubts the ALS diagnosis entirely
Hamilton keeps House off the case
Hamilton offers Foreman partnership in LA at three times salary
Foreman delivers ALS prognosis to Giles
Giles signs Do Not Resuscitate order
House instructs Foreman to start IVIG treatment
IVIG triggers respiratory failure, team can't act due to DNR
House arrives and intubates Giles, violating DNR order
House is served restraining order and battery charges
House's lawyer argues DNR invalid due to low thyroid affecting judgment
House notices judge's clubbed fingers, advises medical consultation
Wilson tells House he has 'Rubik's Complex' - needs to solve puzzle not save patient
Cameron suggests Giles may have had stroke during intubation
MRI reveals blood clot in brain
Foreman performs embolectomy, Giles regains arm movement
House realizes arteriovenous malformation caused paralysis
Surgery removes AVM, Giles walks out with cane
Giles gives House his trumpet
Foreman declines Hamilton's job offer and stays with House

episodes.theme: the cost of being right

interactions.description: all storylines explore what price characters pay for their convictions - House risks legal consequences for medical truth, Foreman chooses growth over money, Wilson challenges his enabling friendship
House's violation of the DNR order serves both his diagnostic obsession and his refusal to accept administrative constraints

events.event: Victoria Matsen arrives as Jane Doe with suspected overdose, toxicology clean
Foreman dismisses Victoria as faking seizures for free meals
Wilson vehemently disagrees and asks House to take the case as personal favor
Foreman finds dangerously low glucose during seizure, undermining his theory
Team discovers mass on Victoria's ovary
House orders tuberculosis treatment on chance mass is tuberculoma
Biopsy confirms tuberculosis and treatment begins
House bribes police officer and learns Victoria was tasered twice
House tests thigh numbness, finds no pain response but toe reaction
Symptoms point to rabies from bat exposure, swab confirms diagnosis
Foreman receives rabies vaccine after patient bit him
House traces Victoria's identity through surgical pin serial number
Victoria's backstory revealed: car accident killed husband Paul and son James
Wilson reveals he has homeless brother he hasn't seen in nine years
Foreman sits at Victoria's bedside pretending to be Paul, tells her he forgives her
Victoria dies

episodes.theme: the weight of judgment and forgiveness

interactions.description: all three storylines explore judgment and redemption — House solving the unsolvable case, Foreman overcoming his prejudice, Wilson confronting his guilt about his brother
both Foreman and Wilson are transformed by their connection to Victoria's homelessness

events.event: Keith Foster crashes his father's Porsche while coughing blood
Cameron notes Keith's bleeding began before the crash
Cuddy challenges House to go one week without Vicodin
House negotiates stakes: no clinic duty if he succeeds
Wilson reveals the bet was his idea to test House's dependency
Cameron hypothesizes lupus for Keith's condition
Chase suspects drug use as cause of Keith's symptoms
House considers lymphoma as potential diagnosis
Keith develops hemolytic anemia and continues bleeding
House suffers tremors, insomnia, and impaired focus from withdrawal
House's team begins openly doubting his judgment
Keith hallucinates his dead pet cat and suffers rectal bleeding
House smashes his hand with a metal pestle to distract from leg pain
Wilson treats House's broken finger and lies about the cause
House orders Foreman and Chase to dig up Keith's dead cat
Cat necropsy reveals evidence of termite infestation
House realizes Keith has acute naphthalene toxicity from termites
House bursts into OR to halt planned transplant and explains diagnosis
Keith's father punches House but accepts the explanation
Keith recovers from naphthalene toxicity
House tells Wilson he acknowledges addiction but won't quit Vicodin

episodes.theme: the cost of truth and self-knowledge

interactions.description: both storylines explore the painful process of uncovering hidden truths — Keith's toxicity and House's dependency
the audience knows Wilson orchestrated the bet while House initially believes it was Cuddy's idea
House's withdrawal symptoms directly impair his diagnostic abilities, forcing him to find creative solutions

events.event: Hank Wiggen's arm snaps completely while filming anti-drug commercial
Wilson diagnoses severe osteopenia, tells House bones too degraded for surgery
Team suspects steroids, Chase obtains urine sample that tests negative
Hank develops kidney failure, condition worsens
House notices Hank gained twenty-five pounds in Japanese league
Team determines Hank needs kidney transplant, Lola offers to donate
Labs reveal Lola is pregnant, donation would require abortion
Hank refuses to let Lola abort, attempts suicide to prevent transplant
House notices Lola hasn't reacted to strong smells, discovers her anosmia
House connects anosmia with Hank's symptoms, recognizes cadmium poisoning pattern
Hank admits to secretly smoking marijuana throughout recovery
Chase determines marijuana grown in cadmium-contaminated soil from Japan dealer
Chelation treatment begins, transplant and abortion unnecessary
House falsifies records to list Addison's disease, protecting Hank from drug violation
Foreman goes on date with pharmaceutical representative
House takes Cameron to monster truck rally with extra tickets
Wilson has dinner with constitutional lawyer Stacy

episodes.theme: the cost of secrets and recovery

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters hiding truth — Hank's continued drug use, House's record falsification, and the ambiguous nature of House and Cameron's outing
House's decision to falsify medical records directly connects solving the case with his pattern of bending rules

events.event: Gabriel Reilich collapses at home after developing cough, fever, and green sputum
Cuddy pressures House to take the case because Gabriel's father is a major donor
Gabriel tells the team he believes he's cursed after a Ouija board spelled his name
Gabriel admits to entering the abandoned building with friends
Team treats for pneumonia but Gabriel's condition worsens with unexplained rash
House invites Rowan Chase to consult on the case
Chase becomes furious that House contacted his estranged father
Team discovers anthrax spores in animal hair insulation from abandoned house
House investigates Jeffrey's background and discovers he lied about being a fighter pilot
House learns Jeffrey actually spent two years at an ashram in India
House discovers Rowan is visiting Wilson for an oncology consultation, not a conference
House learns Rowan has stage-four lung cancer with three months to live
House concludes Jeffrey transmitted leprosy to Gabriel, compromising his immune system
Gabriel receives targeted treatment for both anthrax and leprosy and improves
Rowan refuses to tell Chase about his cancer diagnosis
House reluctantly agrees to keep Rowan's cancer secret from Chase
Chase attempts to reconcile with Rowan over coffee
Rowan departs for Australia without revealing his diagnosis to Chase
Edward Vogler appears in background of hospital operations

episodes.theme: the medical consequences of family secrets and deception

interactions.description: both storylines explore how family deception causes harm — Jeffrey's lies about his past endanger Gabriel's health, while Rowan's secrecy about his cancer prevents reconciliation with Chase
House and Wilson know Rowan is dying, but Chase doesn't, creating tension in House's decision to keep the secret

events.event: Carly Forlano collapses with leg paralysis during a business meeting
House orders an angiogram to investigate the paralysis
Chase allows radiology resident to scan the wrong leg
House catches the error by noticing backward-oriented toes in the image
Corrected angiogram comes back clean, deepening the puzzle
Team runs muscle biopsy and other tests, all negative
Carly goes into respiratory arrest from pulmonary edema
Echocardiogram confirms severe congestive heart failure
House notices psychological signs and fine cuts on Carly's body
House confronts Carly with bulimia diagnosis and ipecac poisoning
House presents only heart failure to transplant committee, withholding bulimia
Wilson sits on committee and is suspicious but cannot prove House is lying
Carly receives heart transplant and survives
Vogler donates one hundred million dollars and becomes chairman
Vogler identifies House's department as expensive vanity project
Vogler sees House's defiance as liability and begins maneuvering for leverage
Chase's X-ray error makes him vulnerable to Vogler's manipulation
Vogler recognizes opportunity to use Chase as insider against House

episodes.theme: the cost of truth

interactions.description: all storylines explore deception and withholding truth — House hides bulimia from committee, Vogler conceals his agenda, Chase will hide his allegiance
Chase's vulnerability from the medical error becomes Vogler's leverage point
Wilson suspects House is lying but doesn't know the specific truth about bulimia

events.event: Joey Arnello collapses into a coma before testifying in federal court
Judge orders Princeton-Plainsboro to determine if Joey is faking
House examines Joey and confirms real coma with Glasgow Coma Scale
Bill Arnello hovers and works to delay Joey's recovery
Team discovers Joey has hepatitis C and rapidly worsening liver
Chase finds abnormally high estrogen levels
Joey briefly recovers but collapses again after eating steak
House notices pattern and proposes ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency
Investigation reveals Joey taking Chai Hu and herbal estrogen supplements
House confronts Bill about Joey being gay and the poisonous supplements
Bill slaps Chase when Chase mentions hepatitis C transmission vector
Bill accepts the truth and harmful supplements are discontinued
Joey recovers and Bill calls federal agents to make a deal
Vogler pressures Cuddy to fire House
Vogler forces compromise requiring House to fire one fellow
House suspects someone on his team is reporting to Vogler
House sets traps with different information for each fellow
Cuddy tries to protect House's department and the donation
Federal agents question Vogler about prematurely certifying Joey

episodes.theme: the cost of truth

interactions.description: all storylines explore the price of revealing uncomfortable truths — Joey's sexuality, Vogler's corruption, the spy's identity
Vogler's pressure on House creates the spy investigation and firing ultimatum

events.event: Ten-year-old Jessica suffers a heart attack on the playground and is admitted
Chase repeatedly attributes Jessica's symptoms to obesity
Jessica admits to stealing diet pills from a pharmacy
Jessica develops warfarin-induced skin necrosis across her chest
Mother consents to double mastectomy as last resort but begs for another explanation
House re-examines growth charts and realizes Jessica's stunted height
House diagnoses Cushing's syndrome caused by pituitary tumor
Foreman and Chase spot the pituitary tumor on MRI together
Surgical team removes tumor, sparing Jessica the mastectomy
Cuddy informs House he must fire one of his three fellows
House decides to fire Chase and goes to Cuddy
Vogler refuses to allow House to fire Chase
Vogler tells House to pick someone else to fire
Cameron updates her resume in preparation for being fired
All three fellows evaluate each other as competitors rather than collaborators

episodes.theme: betrayal and survival

interactions.description: all storylines explore hidden truths — Jessica's hidden condition, Chase's hidden betrayal, and the team's hidden vulnerabilities
the audience learns Chase is Vogler's informant while the other fellows remain unaware
Vogler's refusal to let House fire Chase reveals Chase's betrayal and fractures team dynamics

events.event: Senator Wright collapses at campaign fundraiser, vomiting and falling down stairs
Vogler pushes House to take Wright's case due to political backing
Vogler offers deal: treat senator and promote Viopril speech for dropping firing demand
House reluctantly agrees to Vogler's deal
House notices Wright's absent patellar reflex indicating neurological problem
Foreman finds scar on Wright's tongue, House deduces childhood epilepsy
MRI reveals brain lesion, biopsy confirms toxoplasmosis
Wright tests positive for HIV, team suspects AIDS
House orders second HIV test which comes back negative
House connects childhood epilepsy treatment and EBV to diagnose CVID
Immunoglobulin treatment produces improvement, confirming CVID diagnosis
House sabotages Vogler's speech, exposing Viopril as generic drug scam
House jokes about his Vicodin consumption during speech
Vogler becomes furious, deal is dead, demands House fire Cameron or Foreman
Cameron goes to House's apartment and resigns to spare him the choice
Cameron tells House her unreciprocated feelings make it impossible to stay
Chase admits to House he has been feeding information to Vogler
House tells Chase he already knew about the betrayal

episodes.theme: the price of integrity

interactions.description: all storylines explore the cost of standing up for principles versus compromising for security
Vogler's corporate control directly conflicts with House's diagnostic independence, culminating in the speech sabotage
House knows Chase is the informant but doesn't reveal this knowledge until the end

events.event: Naomi Randolph passes out while driving, nearly causing a crash
House and team discover impaired kidney and liver function plus lung mass
House diagnoses small-cell lung cancer with paraneoplastic syndrome
Team recommends immediate Cesarean section for chemotherapy
Naomi begs to wait one more week, citing her history of child loss
Sean authorizes early delivery despite Naomi's pleas
House attempts to get Naomi into clinical trial to delay Cesarean
Vogler blocks House's access to trial resources as punishment
Cesarean is performed successfully, both mother and baby survive
Vogler convenes board meeting to revoke House's tenure
Vogler itemizes House's transgressions and threatens donation withdrawal
Wilson defends House passionately, casting lone dissenting vote
Vogler retaliates by removing Wilson from the board
Wilson is forced to resign from hospital entirely
House is uncharacteristically shaken by Wilson's sacrifice
Cuddy reverses support for Vogler in final board session
Cuddy persuades board that Vogler's agenda is incompatible with hospital mission
Board votes Vogler out, Wilson is reinstated

episodes.theme: the cost of principles

interactions.description: all three storylines explore sacrifice for principles — Naomi's husband choosing her life over baby's odds, Wilson sacrificing his career for House, Cuddy sacrificing the donation for hospital independence
Vogler's power play directly interferes with House's medical case when he blocks access to clinical trial
all storylines merge in the board room climax where Wilson's loyalty, Vogler's corporate agenda, and House's independence collide

events.event: Mary Carroll experiences blurred vision during diving but completes her dive
Meningitis outbreak floods Princeton-Plainsboro with hundreds of patients
House notices Mary's neck pain pattern is inconsistent with meningitis
Lumbar puncture comes back negative for infection
Foreman discovers Mary is bleeding from her mouth
Chase and Foreman insert endoscope but find no gastrointestinal lesions
Mary suffers absence seizure with EEG spikes
Transcranial ultrasound reveals brain bleeding, emergency surgery relieves pressure
House discovers Mary is pregnant through abdominal ultrasound
House determines pregnancy triggered thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
Mary consents to abortion under New Jersey law without parental notification
House visits Cameron's apartment to offer her job back
Cameron asks why House wants her back, slams door at generic answer
Cameron agrees to return on condition House takes her on a date
House resists but eventually accepts Cameron's dating condition
Cuddy manages hospital-wide meningitis emergency with competence
House, Foreman, and Chase work without Cameron during crisis

episodes.theme: acknowledging personhood beyond function

interactions.description: both storylines explore seeing people as whole beings rather than just their function — Mary as a person capable of making her own medical decisions, Cameron as a person deserving acknowledgment beyond her diagnostic skills
Cameron's absence forces the team to function differently, affecting Foreman and Chase's roles and dynamics

events.event: House snaps at Harvey Park in the clinic, mistaking apple juice for urine
Harvey suffers a stroke in front of House when he returns to apologize
House agrees to take Harvey's case to avoid a lawsuit
Team explores standard stroke explanations but finds no aneurysm during surgery
Foreman discovers Annette choking Harvey in his hospital bed and pulls her off
Harvey insists Annette was not hurting him despite the choking
Chase identifies Annette as a dominatrix and Harvey as an asphyxiaphiliac
Chase mentions he has encountered asphyxiaphilia before with a former girlfriend
Team cannot perform MRI due to Harvey's steel jaw plate from reconstructive surgery
House notices the smell from Harvey's mouth and diagnoses fulminating osteomyelitis
House confirms diagnosis by inserting needle into Harvey's jaw and drawing out pus
Harvey undergoes surgery to remove infected bone and recovers
House warns Harvey and Annette that asphyxiation must stop or it will kill him
House and Cameron go on their date at an upscale restaurant
Cameron tells House to skip small talk and say what he honestly thinks of her
House delivers devastating assessment that Cameron lives under delusion she can fix imperfect things
House tells Cameron she married a dying man and now wants him as her next charity case
Cameron is wounded but does not break, returns to team the next day
Wilson observes the House-Cameron date and offers wry commentary to House
Hospital adjusts to loss of hundred million dollars after Vogler's departure
Cuddy manages budget fallout from Vogler situation

episodes.theme: the cost of honesty

interactions.description: both storylines involve House confronting uncomfortable truths — Harvey's sexual practices and Cameron's psychological motivations
Wilson sees House's emotional isolation more clearly than House himself does

patches.reason: Events about hospital financial fallout from Vogler's departure don't fit existing storylines

events.event: Cuddy pressures House into substituting for a sick professor and lecturing medical students
House encounters Stacy Warner, his ex-girlfriend whom he has not seen in five years
Stacy asks House to examine her husband Mark, who has been experiencing intermittent symptoms
House reviews Mark's file and says he does not appear to be ill
The encounter with Stacy visibly rattles House
House presents the first case: a farmer with leg pain who lied about a dog bite
House reveals the farmer's leg was amputated due to delayed treatment from the lie
House presents the second case: a volleyball player who collapses during practice
House reveals the volleyball player had bone cancer but was caught early enough for successful surgery
House initially presents the third patient as Carmen Electra, drawing laughs from students
House's team members realize the third patient is House himself from five years earlier
House reveals he experienced sudden, excruciating pain from a blood clot causing muscle death
House explains doctors initially dismissed his complaints as drug-seeking behavior
Cameron identifies the mechanism: dying muscle releases myoglobin that shuts down kidneys
House reveals he refused amputation and chose risky bypass surgery
House reveals Stacy authorized a middle-ground procedure while he was in a coma, overriding his wishes
House explains he could not forgive Stacy for the decision, ending their relationship
The lecture reveals Cuddy was the attending physician during House's infarction

episodes.theme: the cost of truth and the burden of choice

interactions.description: all storylines explore how past decisions and their consequences shape present relationships and professional identity
House's medical crisis five years ago is the origin point for his current pain, his breakup with Stacy, and his complex relationship with Cuddy
the lecture format serves as a structural device to reveal backstory through case presentations

events.event: Stacy asks House to help her husband Mark, who refuses to see House
Stacy arranges dinner where House shows up unannounced
House drugs Mark's food to get him to the hospital, violating consent
Initial tests come back negative but Mark's symptoms worsen
House orders exploratory surgery and spots abdominal tremors on tape
Team suspects Alzheimer's but rules it out
Mark describes false honeymoon memory to Paris during brain scan
Stacy reveals they never went to Paris, confirming Mark's delusions
House notes Mark's photosensitivity and diagnoses acute intermittent porphyria
House proposes dangerous barbiturate test to confirm diagnosis
Mark refuses the test, exercising his right as competent patient
Stacy persuades House to administer test against Mark's wishes
House's team tries to stop him but he proceeds with the test
House stabs Mark in bladder with syringe to collect urine during attack
Urine tests positive for porphyria, House prescribes treatment and Mark recovers
Cameron tells House she realizes he cannot love her because he loves Stacy
Stacy confesses she is not over House but cannot be with him
Stacy kisses House and walks away after saying she felt lonely with him
Cuddy offers Stacy position as general counsel to oversee Mark's recovery
House consents to Stacy working at the hospital

episodes.theme: the cost of love and sacrifice

interactions.description: all storylines explore sacrifice and consent — House violates Mark's consent to save him, just as Stacy violated House's consent years ago; Cameron sacrifices her romantic hopes
the audience knows House loves Stacy while Cameron pursues him, creating painful dramatic tension
the medical case becomes the vehicle for resolving House and Stacy's relationship — saving her husband forces them to confront their past
